title: Podman caching (?) Dockerfile?
body:
### Issue Description

podman build is using an old version of Dockerfile when building

### Steps to reproduce the issue

(I don't think this is consistent, so I'll just describe what happened to me)

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. I had a Dockerfile where I was using --link on a COPY instruction, but that doesn't work on Podman.
2. I deleted the --link from the instructions
3. When I run the build, it's still using the instruction with --link even when it's not in the Dockerfile anymore.


### Describe the results you received

This is my Dockerfile now:

```
ARG NODE_VERSION=18.18.0
FROM node:${NODE_VERSION}-slim

WORKDIR "/server"

COPY ./server ./

RUN npm install

WORKDIR "/svelte"

COPY ./svelte ./

RUN npm install
RUN npm run build

WORKDIR "/server"

ENV NODE_ENV="production"
ENV PORT=3000

EXPOSE 3000

CMD [ "npm", "run", "start" ]
```

This is the error shown:

```
[2/3] STEP 3/5: COPY --link ./server/package.json ./
Error: building at STEP "COPY --link ./server/package.json ./": COPY only supports the --chmod=<permissions> --chown=<uid:gid> and the --from=<image|stage> flags
```


### Describe the results you expected

I was expecting it to build without any issues.

comments.body: Sorry turns out I was by mistake looking at 2 different project folders. That got me confuse.
